title: Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LVII
content:
You smile upon your friend to-day,
    To-day his ills are over;
    You hearken to the lover's say,
    And happy is the lover.

    'Tis late to hearken, late to smile,
    But better late than never:
    I shall have lived a little while
    Before I die for ever.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Alfred Edward Housman delivers a powerful performance in "Poems From "A Shropshire Lad" - LVII"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
